programme.title: It Takes a Killer
programme.desc: When a law school student is strangled to death in her bed, detectives discover that someone she knew extremely well had been secretly watching her for months by videotaping her through her second story window.

programme.title: It Takes a Killer
programme.desc: A clerk, turned prolific serial killer, flies under the radar for years, but when law enforcement finally tracks him down, they discover he's far more demented than they ever could have imagined.

programme.title: It Takes a Killer

programme.sub-title: The Preacher, His Mistress and Murder

programme.desc: After a doctor's wife is found dead in her bathtub, a toxicology report reveals lethal levels of painkillers; at trial, the doctor's dark side comes out.

programme.desc: A child's comment leads police to find the remains of nine young women at Fred and Rosemary West's Gloucester, England, home.

programme.title: Corrupt Crimes
programme.desc: A relative's traffic violation helps police to find the murderer of a teen girl, 25 years before.

programme.title: Corrupt Crimes

programme.title: Corrupt Crimes
programme.desc: A strangler has killed several elderly women in Mexico City; there is no connection between the victims and no discernible motive; after the police investigation comes to nothing, the killer is suddenly caught red-handed.

programme.title: Victim to Verdict: the Burn Pile Murder
programme.desc: A prominent Georgia attorney is dead, and his remains found in a burn pile; wife and son blame each other amid family feuds and affairs; a juror explains the difficult decision of who is guilty of the multi-million dollar estate.

programme.title: Victim to Verdict: the Matriarch Mastermind Murder
programme.desc: After Dan Markel's murder by two hitmen, his former mother-in-law, Donna Adelson, is now accused of orchestrating the plot; with her son and his girlfriend already convicted, prosecutors allege Donna was the mastermind.

programme.title: Victim to Verdict: Murdaugh Family Murders
programme.desc: Once prominent South Carolina attorney Alex Murdaugh's life begins to unravel after the murders of his wife, Maggie and youngest son Paul as an investigation uncovers years of drug abuse and theft of millions of dollars.

programme.title: Victim To Verdict - Johnny Depp V Amber Heard
programme.desc: Hollywood star Johnny Depp's career suffers after his ex-wife Amber Heard wrote an op-ed claiming to be a victim of sexual violence; what follows is a civil trial that captures the attention of the public.

programme.title: Victim to Verdict: The Delphi Murders
programme.desc: Two young girls murdered and a small town shaken; with no DNA, no weapon and a suspect hiding in plain sight, will there be justice for Abby Williams and Libby German?

programme.title: Victim to Verdict: the Idaho Four
programme.desc: Four University of Idaho students are stabbed to death in the middle of the night, capturing the world's attention and putting the spotlight on a small-town police department.

programme.desc: A military veteran finds himself facing murder charges for beating his girlfriend to death; now, doctors suspect his traumatic brain injury and PTSD explain his behavior.

programme.title: Butterfly Effect
programme.desc: Possible outcomes if Kong Qui, known in the West as Confucius, had been as opportunistic and corrupt as other officials.

programme.title: Butterfly Effect
programme.sub-title: Genghis Khan: The Empire of the Steppes
programme.desc: The impact the events of his childhood had on shaping the man who would become Genghis Khan, and how the Mongol invasions affected the history of Asia and the psyche of Europe.

programme.title: Butterfly Effect
programme.desc: The possible development of scientific conversation over the last 200 years had Charles Darwin's "On the Origin of Species" never been published.

programme.title: Butterfly Effect
programme.sub-title: Chernobyl: A Reactor Out of Control
programme.desc: The decisions of only a handful of individuals determined the course of the nuclear disaster at Chernobyl and the impact it would have on the world.

programme.title: Butterfly Effect
programme.sub-title: Sekigahara: The Battle of the Samurais
programme.desc: Japan's internal politics and the country's response to external influences can be traced back to the choices of two warlords and a decisive battle during the Edo Period.

programme.title: Butterfly Effect
programme.desc: Albert Einstein's influence changed the face of war and the world when he chose to write to the President of the United States.

programme.title: Butterfly Effect
programme.desc: The untimely death of the Roman Emperor of the Western Empire, and the poor decisions of the Roman Emperor of the Eastern Empire, change the fortunes of the Roman army in its battle with the Goths.

programme.title: Butterfly Effect
programme.sub-title: Internet: The Origins of the Web

programme.title: Butterfly Effect

programme.desc: Prospero summons other fictionals in order to retrieve parts of his staff; the librarians find themselves pitted against Frankenstein's monster and Sherlock Holmes' nemesis Moriarty; Flynn and Baird have a disagreement.

programme.title: Zombie House Flipping
programme.desc: The team finds a hurricane-damaged zombie that already has a buyer lined up who's willing to pay market value for a good flip, but he wants the house ready in one month; the team races to remove piles of debris and fends off rainy weather.

programme.title: Treyvaud Travels
programme.desc: Treyvaud travels to Quinns pub in Dungannon to look back at his entire series. We get to see hilarious unseen footage, bloopers and some of Treyvaud’s most favorite moments of his amazing travels.

programme.title: Treyvaud Travels
programme.desc: Treyvaud travels to Killarney and cooks a healthy dish on a jaunting car whilst being whisked through the stunning national Park. Then hikes up Cardiac Hill before having a pint in The Killarney Brewing Company.

programme.title: Treyvaud Travels
programme.desc: Treyvaud travels to the town of Kenmare to see how to smoke the world famous Star Seafood Smoked salmon, then cooks an incredible dish in an original famine Cottage in the stunning Gleninchaquin Park.

programme.title: Treyvaud Travels
programme.desc: Treyvaud travels to Long Meadow Cider company to see how father and son team make their incredible cider.

programme.title: Treyvaud Travels
programme.desc: Treyvaud travels to meet master smoker Paul Curry, later cooking a great dish while gaining exclusive access to one of Irelands most stunning private castles. Then its off to Corick House Hotel & Spa for a spot of foraging and more great recipes!

programme.title: Treyvaud Travels
programme.desc: Treyvaud travels to the breath taking Dursey Island in Cork, home of Irelands only cable car, to learn how to cook fresh scones and jam. After, he cooks up an amazing lobster dish with the Wild Atlantic Way as his backdrop!

programme.title: Treyvaud Travels
programme.desc: Treyvaud travels to the stunning coastal town of Ballycastle in County Antrim, where he visits the beautifully located Ballycastle farmers market and then cooks a fantastic scallop dish on board one of the local boats.

programme.title: Treyvaud Travels
programme.desc: Treyvaud travels to the stunning coastal town of Ballycastle in County Antrim, where he visits the beautifully located Ballycastle farmers market and then cooks a fantastic scallop dish on board one of the local boats.

programme.title: Treyvaud Travels
programme.desc: Treyvaud travels to the mighty Thomond Park to cook on the pitch of Munster RFC and meets world rugby legend Doug Howlett. Then it’s off to the stunning Adare Village where Paul meets up with his old pal Wade Murphy of 1826 Restaurant.

programme.title: Treyvaud Travels
programme.desc: Treyvaud travels to Enniskillen to meet Pat O’Doherty and his famous pigs. Paul is given a master class in butchery and then travels by boat to see the world famous ‘black bacon’ pigs run wild on their very own private island!

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '70s - Cloris Leachman (2/1/78)
programme.desc: Who's racked up more Emmys than anyone except Julia Louis Dreyfuss and Carl Reiner? Cloris Leachman. She talks to Johnny about growing up in Des Moines and doing live TV.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '70s - Goldie Hawn (2/22/78)
programme.desc: The bikini'd star of Laugh-in and Oscar winner Goldie Hawn talks about giving birth. And what's the big deal with King Tut? Buck Henry, who created Get Smart and wrote The Graduate has some thoughts.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '70s - Bruce Dern (3/2/78)
programme.desc: Even stars get stiffed. Ask Bruce Dern. Comedian Robert Klein reports on making a movie with Burt Reynolds, and Dr. Carl Sagan bashes Star Wars for strangling science.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '70s - Burt Reynolds (8/2/78)
programme.desc: Pin up guy Burt Reynolds grouses about look-a-likes who impersonate him at public functions. Susan Sarandon demos relaxation techniques and reads Johnny's palm.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '70s - Angie Dickinson (4/4/79)
programme.desc: Rat pack darling Angie Dickinson chats about self defense training. Johnny practices the Heimlich maneuver on her with coaching from its developer, Dr. Henry Heimlich.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '70s - Burt Reynolds (6/21/79)
programme.desc: Celebrities at the pump; Burt Reynolds talks about waiting his turn. He also fesses up to coveting an Oscar. Jimmy Carter's mom, Miss Lillian, admits she doesn't like sleeping at the White House.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '70s - Burt Reynolds (6/21/79)
programme.desc: Celebrities at the pump; Burt Reynolds talks about waiting his turn. He also fesses up to coveting an Oscar. Jimmy Carter's mom, Miss Lillian, admits she doesn't like sleeping at the White House.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '70s - Bruce Dern (12/20/79)
programme.desc: Acting icon Bruce Dern talks about working with Ann-Margret on Middle Age Crazy, and David Letterman scores laughs describing his visit to a nude beach and his dog's love life.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '70s - Angie Dickinson (12/30/80)
programme.desc: Actress Angie Dickinson, who's made over 50 films, chats about New Year's Eve parties, New Year's resolutions, her first date and coping with depression.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '70s - Alan Alda (5/19/81)
programme.desc: M*A*S*H* star Alan Alda discusses his career and the impact of celebrity on his life. Comedian David Brenner riffs on health food, controlling his weight and eating in New York.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '70s - Bo Derek (7/24/81)
programme.desc: TV star Michael Landon gets sentimental over Bonanza; watching episodes dubbed in Japanese and promoting the show at county fairs. Actress Bo Derek talks about vine-swinging for Tarzan, the Ape Man.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '70s - Angie Dickinson (7/31/81)
programme.desc: Show biz veteran Angie Dickinson talks about men who whistle at women, Tommy Lasorda chats about losing weight, and Dom DeLuise dishes on doing an off-Broadway play with Barbra Streisand.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '70s - John Travolta (8/6/81)
programme.desc: Actor/pilot John Travolta on flying lessons, dropping out of high school and getting his first part. Humorist Erma Bombeck complains that "housewives" on commercials don't look like housewives.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '70s - Burt Reynolds (2/11/82)
programme.desc: Heartthrob Burt Reynolds chats about his Cosmo centerfold and comedian Richard Lewis jokes about his dandruff, driving in LA and growing up in New Jersey.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '70s - Burt Reynolds (2/11/82)
programme.desc: Heartthrob Burt Reynolds chats about his Cosmo centerfold and comedian Richard Lewis jokes about his dandruff, driving in LA and growing up in New Jersey.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '70s - Roger Moore (3/30/82)
programme.desc: With five Bond movies under his belt, Sir Roger Moore says he'll keep going. Having just won the Oscar for Reds, Maureen Stapleton wonders aloud how it will impact her career.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '70s - Jodie Foster (3/4/83)
programme.desc: Yale student and movie star Jodie Foster chats about college life, John Hinckley and working with Peter O'Toole. Tim Conway describes the brochure he asks his prospective dates to read.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '70s - Donald Sutherland (5/13/83)
programme.desc: Autograph hounds watch out: Donald Sutherland has a policy on handling signature seekers. Comic actor Martin Mull answers his fan mail and shows off his artwork.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '70s - Cloris Leachman (5/9/84)
programme.desc: Eight-time Emmy winner Cloris Leachman reveals her favorite prize --mother of the year -- awarded by a club in New York. 91year old Olympic athlete Abel Kivat discusses how he keeps in shape.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '70s - Miss Piggy (7/12/84)
programme.desc: The epitome of beauty and elegance, Miss Piggy extols the joys of learning French. Comedian Howie Mandel covers his seat with plastic because he says he wants to feel like he's at his mom's.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '70s - Peter Fonda (1/4/85)
programme.desc: Actor/screenwriter Peter Fonda opens up about being compared to his father and sister. Charles Nelson Reilly recalls his dad pouring cheap bourbon into fancy bottles to hide their poverty.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '70s - Candice Bergen (3/21/85)
programme.desc: Soon-to-be star of Murphy Brown, Candice Bergen chats about her marriage and how rude the French are. Comic actor Tim Reid says he was fired from show for not being "black enough."

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '70s - Candice Bergen (3/21/85)
programme.desc: Soon-to-be star of Murphy Brown, Candice Bergen chats about her marriage and how rude the French are. Comic actor Tim Reid says he was fired from show for not being "black enough."

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '70s - Gary Busey (8/23/85)
programme.desc: Oscar nom'd for playing the title role in The Buddy Holly Story, Gary Busey has acted in over 150 movies. He talks about his early rocker days playing in a band at Disneyland and Knott's Berry Farm.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '70s - Angie Dickinson (1/2/86)
programme.desc: Catch outrageous flirting as Angie Dickinson denies rumors about her and singer Julio Iglesias. Then she gets all sweet and cozy on Johnny. "We are not strangers," she tells the audience.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '70s - Burt Reynolds (11/5/86)
programme.desc: The always virile Burt Reynolds reflects on his 1973 Cosmo poster, and his five-year romance with actress Loni Anderson. The maturing boy-toy-turned-movie-star is penning his autobiography.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '70s - George C. Scott (11/3/87)
programme.desc: The first actor to refuse an Oscar, George C. Scott for the title role in Patton, chats about his love of theatre, the many bombs in which he's appeared and turning 60 years old.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '70s - Burt Reynolds (2/25/88)
programme.desc: Taking up the pen, Burt Reynolds discusses his work on his autobiography. He comments on his streak of box office flops. Burt warmly welcomes Eddy Edwards, Britain's one-man Olympic Ski Jumping team.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '70s - Cybill Shepherd (6/8/89)
programme.desc: SNL alum Martin Short admits he was a singer before taking up comedy. Shows a clip from his HBO special. Model-turned-TV-star, Cybill Shepherd talks about her kids, breastfeeding and divorce.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '70s - Leonard Nimoy (4/3/91)
programme.desc: Star Trek's Dr. Spock -- Leonard Nimoy - presents Johnny with a pair of Vulcan ears. Comic Bill Maher goes off on the sexual revolution, women's rights and his current romance.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '70s - Leonard Nimoy (4/3/91)
programme.desc: Star Trek's Dr. Spock -- Leonard Nimoy - presents Johnny with a pair of Vulcan ears. Comic Bill Maher goes off on the sexual revolution, women's rights and his current romance.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '70s - Sylvester Stallone (5/2/91)
programme.desc: Sylvester Stallone reflects on being recognizable worldwide and issues in his private life. Actor Orson Bean recalls the early days of TV and makes fun of daytime talk show topics.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '70s - George Segal (11/8/91)
programme.desc: Oscar nom'd George Segal demonstrates playing an old man using prosthetics, glasses and gait. Comedian Drew Carey riffs on his high school reunion, driving a Yugo and his love of bad food.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '70s - Burt Reynolds (3/12/92)
programme.desc: Whipped cream anyone? Burt Reynolds shows a clip of him squirting whipped cream down Johnny's trousers. Burt then shoves a can in his own pants and sprays his crotch.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '70s - Lily Tomlin (12/3/75)
programme.desc: Comic actress Lily Tomlin talks about her family's reaction to her one-woman concert and recounts her brother's arrest in Detroit for being in a car with a black woman.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '70s - Sylvester Stallone (5/21/82)
programme.desc: Rocky star Sylvester Stallone brings son Sage to meet Johnny. Describes getting kicked out of several schools, and chats about stardom with fellow guest Steve Martin.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '70s - Tony Randall (6/21/77)
programme.desc: Felix Unger reborn! TV veteran Tony Randall chats about his new role as a Philadelphia judge on The Tony Randall Show. Also tells Johnny he's quit the booze.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '70s - Tony Randall (6/21/77)
programme.desc: Felix Unger reborn! TV veteran Tony Randall chats about his new role as a Philadelphia judge on The Tony Randall Show. Also tells Johnny he's quit the booze.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '70s - Tony Randall (11/21/79)
programme.desc: Felix Unger gets sexy? Actor Tony Randall spills the beans on his early experiences with girls and the first time he bought condoms. Also opines on sex education classes.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '70s - Teri Garr (3/25/83)
programme.desc: Comic actress Teri Garr creates a stir on Johnny's set in her see-through blouse. Shows a clip from her Oscar-nom'd performance in Tootsie, and parses success in show biz vs. recognition as an actress.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '80s - Susan Sarandon (5/9/74)
programme.desc: Movie icon Susan Sarandon talks about her bad luck, plus Jack Palance and Joanna Cassidy stop by.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '80s - Adrienne Barbeau (12/12/74)
programme.desc: Adrienne Barbeau has received some strange Christmas presents! Plus, Johnny interviews Richard Harris and Bob Hope.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '80s - George Peppard (7/7/76)
programme.desc: George Peppard gets relatable when he reveals that he doesn't like himself when he looks in the mirror. Plus, Charles O. Finley talks about orange baseballs, Geoffrey Bourne discusses his book and Charlie Callas does standup.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '80s - George Peppard (7/7/76)
programme.desc: George Peppard gets relatable when he reveals that he doesn't like himself when he looks in the mirror. Plus, Charles O. Finley talks about orange baseballs, Geoffrey Bourne discusses his book and Charlie Callas does standup.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '80s - George Miller (4/7/78)
programme.desc: George Miller does standup, Robert Mitchum talks about his marijuana conviction, and Dick Van Patten and stuntman Dennis Madalone appear.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '80s - Sally Field (2/20/79)
programme.desc: Sally Field details her relationship with Burt Reynolds. Plus, Buddy Hackett reveals his arguing strategy with his wife.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '80s - James Woods (1/8/81)
programme.desc: James Woods has some marriage advice. McLean Stevenson and Bum Phillips also have something to say about wives and matrimony.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '80s - Albert Brooks (4/9/81)
programme.desc: Albert Brooks talks puppy love, Susan Sarandon talks clam shucking and Carnac the Magnificent works his magic.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '80s - James Woods (10/8/81)
programme.desc: James Woods talks body casts, Peter Billingsley plans to quit acting when he turns 18 and Martin Mull takes an audience poll.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '80s - Hulk Hogan (6/15/82)
programme.desc: Hulkomania takes over The Tonight Show! How do George Miller and Brooke Shields follow that??

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '80s - Hulk Hogan (6/15/82)
programme.desc: Hulkomania takes over The Tonight Show! How do George Miller and Brooke Shields follow that??

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '80s - Sally Field (12/17/82)
programme.desc: Sally Field wants to shed her "cute little girl" image. Plus, things get Christmassy on the Tonight Show set.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '80s - Brooke Shields (5/17/83)
programme.desc: Brooke Shields discusses Sahara, Albert Brooks goes on a mild tirade and Johnny jokes about President Reagan.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '80s - Carrie Fisher (7/29/83)
programme.desc: Carrie Fisher talks Return of the Jedi and Garry Shandling gets more than a few laughs.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '80s - Susan Sarandon (1/13/84)
programme.desc: Susan Sarandon, Brooke Shields and Carol Wayne all stop by the couch, and Johnny talks about Playboy.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '80s - Ed Begley Jr. (2/23/84)
programme.desc: Ed Begley Jr. discusses starting out in the business. Suzanne Pleshette and Michael Davis also appear.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '80s - Farrah Fawcett (9/27/84)
programme.desc: Small screen icon Farrah Fawcett discusses her new TV movie, and Ryan O'Neal is obsessed with her.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.sub-title: The Johnny Carson Show: Hollywood Icons Of The '80s - Ed Begley, Jr. (9/28/84)
programme.desc: Ed Begley Jr. returns and is joined by Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom's Kate Capshaw and comedian Bobby Kelton.
